confidence.disclosure: Reconstructed from Hasan Beg Rumlu's Ahsan al-Tavarikh, Khwandamir's Habib al-Siyar, and Qasem Beg Hayati's Tarikh. The period of concealment in Lahijan, the rapid early campaigns to Ardabil and Tabriz (1499-1501), and the fateful defeat at Chaldiran (August 1514) are all well dated. Dates for some later eastern campaigns rely on general chronology rather than specific entries; month-precision is uncommon outside the Chaldiran campaign.

sources: Roger Savory, Iran Under the Safavids (1980)
Andrew Newman, Safavid Iran: Rebirth of a Persian Empire (2006)

journey.event: Born at Ardabil on 17 July 1487, son of the Safavid sheikh Haydar and grandson of Uzun Hasan of the Aq Qoyunlu.
Taken at age six by loyal Safavid followers to hide in Lahijan in the forests of Gilan after his father's death and brothers' deaths.
Emerged from concealment at age twelve and began to gather Qizilbash followers.
Summoned his followers to assemble at Erzincan in eastern Anatolia to launch his movement.
Invaded Shirvan and defeated its ruler Farrukh Yasar, avenging his father's death.
Defeated the Aq Qoyunlu ruler Alvand Mirza at the Battle of Sharur near Nakhchivan in summer 1501.
Entered Tabriz in triumph and was proclaimed Shah; declared Twelver Shi'ism the state religion.
Defeated the last Aq Qoyunlu ruler Murad at the Battle of Alma Qulaq near Hamadan.
Took Isfahan and absorbed central Persia into the nascent Safavid state.
Accepted the submission of Shiraz and the Fars province.
Added Yazd and the central desert province to the Safavid realm.
Reduced the Kia dynasty of the Caspian uplands around Firuzkuh.
Captured Diyarbakir in the Jazira, extending Safavid power west of the Euphrates.
Entered Baghdad and visited the Shia shrine cities of Najaf, Karbala, Kazimayn, and Samarra.
Prayed at the shrine of Ali ibn Abi Talib in Najaf.
Visited the shrine of Husayn at Karbala, fulfilling Twelver Shia pilgrimage obligations.
Defeated and killed the Uzbek khan Muhammad Shaybani at the Battle of Merv on 2 December 1510.
Entered Herat after the Uzbek collapse and installed a Safavid governor over Khurasan.
Visited the shrine of Imam Ali al-Rida at Mashhad.
Returned to Isfahan to consolidate his rule over western Iran.
Gathered his army at Tabriz as Selim I's Ottoman forces advanced.
Moved northwest to Khoy to intercept the Ottoman army.
Defeated by Selim I at the Battle of Chaldiran on 23 August 1514; Ottoman artillery and janissary firepower destroyed the Qizilbash cavalry charge.
Lost Tabriz to the Ottomans for several weeks before Selim withdrew due to winter.
Withdrew to winter quarters at Dargazin after the Ottoman withdrawal.
Held court at Isfahan during years of depression following the defeat at Chaldiran.
Visited the family shrine at Ardabil.
Died on 23 May 1524 at Saeenqaleh and was buried at the Safavid family shrine in Ardabil.
